Film by Eric Valette

***One Missed Call*** is a 2008 [supernatural horror film](/wiki/Supernatural_horror_film "Supernatural horror film")[[3]](#cite_note-RottenTomatoes-3) directed by Eric Valette and written by [Andrew Klavan](/wiki/Andrew_Klavan "Andrew Klavan"). An international co-production between the United States, the United Kingdom, Japan and Germany,[[1]](#cite_note-lumiere-1) it is a [remake](/wiki/Remake "Remake") of the [2003 Japanese film of the same name](/wiki/One_Missed_Call_(2003_film) "One Missed Call (2003 film)") directed by [Takashi Miike](/wiki/Takashi_Miike "Takashi Miike"), which itself was based on the [Yasushi Akimoto](/wiki/Yasushi_Akimoto "Yasushi Akimoto") novel *Chakushin Ari*. The film stars [Shannyn Sossamon](/wiki/Shannyn_Sossamon "Shannyn Sossamon"), [Edward Burns](/wiki/Edward_Burns "Edward Burns"), [Ana Claudia Talancón](/wiki/Ana_Claudia_Talanc%C3%B3n "Ana Claudia Talancón"), [Ray Wise](/wiki/Ray_Wise "Ray Wise") and [Azura Skye](/wiki/Azura_Skye "Azura Skye").

The film was released in North America on January 4, 2008. Despite being a moderate box office success, the film was overwhelmingly panned by film critics, with many regarding it as the worst [J-horror](/wiki/J-horror "J-horror") remake to be released. It became the worst-reviewed film of 2008, receiving a [0% rating approval on Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/List_of_films_with_a_0%25_rating_on_Rotten_Tomatoes "List of films with a 0% rating on Rotten Tomatoes") and winning a Moldy Tomato Award on the same site.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

A [paramedic](/wiki/Paramedic "Paramedic") rescues a young girl from the blazing Saint Luke's [hospital](/wiki/Hospital "Hospital") before unsuccessfully inquiring about her mother's whereabouts. Sometime afterwards, undergraduate Shelley Baum hears her cat meowing near her house's [koi pond](/wiki/Koi_pond "Koi pond"). After she heads over to investigate, a hand appears and drowns her and her cat. Days later, college students Beth Raymond and Leann Cole discuss Shelley's funeral. Leann's [cellphone](/wiki/Cellphone "Cellphone") rings in a [lullaby](/wiki/Lullaby "Lullaby")-esque [ringtone](/wiki/Ringtone "Ringtone"), with a call from Shelley. She opens it to an eerie [voicemail](/wiki/Voicemail "Voicemail") of herself, dated for June 12 at 10:17 PM. Subsequently, suffering from disturbing [hallucinations](/wiki/Hallucination "Hallucination"), she calls Beth while returning from a study session. Beth rushes to her location but arrives just as Leann falls off an [overpass](/wiki/Overpass "Overpass") and is struck by a train. A red candy pops out of her mouth, and her severed hand dials a number on her phone. At Leann's funeral, her ex-boyfriend Brian Sousa departs after experiencing hallucinations. Outside a [coffee shop](/wiki/Coffeehouse "Coffeehouse"), he shows Beth Leann's post-mortem voicemail, dated minutes away. An [acetylene](/wiki/Acetylene "Acetylene") tank explosion from the adjacent [construction site](/wiki/Construction_site "Construction site") launches debris into the air, and a [rebar](/wiki/Rebar "Rebar") impales Brian's [torso](/wiki/Torso "Torso"). A red candy is ejected from his mouth, and he collapses.

The next day, Beth meets [police](/wiki/Police "Police") [detective](/wiki/Detective "Detective") Jack Andrews, who mentions that his sister Jean interned with Shelley at Saint Luke's and died herself two days prior. They determine the events are interrelated and he provides his contact card. Beth consoles her friend Taylor Anthony, who is distraught by a premonitory feeling of being the next victim, so Beth removes the [batteries](/wiki/Electric_battery "Electric battery") from both their cellphones to disable them. That night, Taylor's cellphone rings, which she opens to a [video](/wiki/Video "Video") of her apparent demise. The following morning, Jack and Beth research geriatric nurse Marie Layton, originator of the calls, and find the [autopsy](/wiki/Autopsy "Autopsy") [report](/wiki/Report "Report") of her eldest daughter Ellie, who died from an acute [asthmatic episode](/wiki/Asthma "Asthma"). The file, mentioning "no [bruising](/wiki/Bruising "Bruising") but evidence of past scars" with an attached [CPS](/wiki/Child_protective_services "Child protective services") file for further consultation, indicates that Jean, a [psychiatric](/wiki/Psychiatric "Psychiatric") nurse, questioned Marie at Saint Luke's and noted nine [admissions](/wiki/Hospital_admission "Hospital admission") between April and May for Ellie and her sister Laurel, concerning several causes, leading Beth to assume that Marie had [FDIA](/wiki/FDIA "FDIA").

Meanwhile, [TV producer](/wiki/TV_producer "TV producer") Ted Summers, who approached Taylor earlier, prepares to record her exorcism, explaining that [spiritual](/wiki/Spirituality "Spirituality") [energy](/wiki/Energy "Energy") operates in the same [electromagnetic spectrum](/wiki/Electromagnetic_spectrum "Electromagnetic spectrum") as [light](/wiki/Light "Light")/[microwaves](/wiki/Microwave "Microwave") and is therefore transmissible via cellular phones from which it manifests as hallucinations. Noticing the show's [advertisement](/wiki/Television_advertisement "Television advertisement") on TV, Beth races to the site, arriving to an unseen [force](/wiki/Ghost "Ghost") fatally choking Taylor. Her phone then sounds with a voicemail dated for tomorrow. Taking Beth home, Jack learns about her past in which her mother was abusive towards her, (in particular would burn her daughter with her cigarette), and her father, aware of his wife's abuse, [hanged himself](/wiki/Hanged_himself "Hanged himself") in their house's attic.

Assuming that finding Marie will settle the matter, she ventures to Saint Luke's and encounters Jack. As they enter an [operating room](/wiki/Operating_room "Operating room"), Marie's spirit ejects and incapacitates Jack. Locked inside, Beth throws a chiming phone across the room, where it knocks the [air duct's](/wiki/Heating,_ventilation,_and_air_conditioning "Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning") grill cover loose, revealing a [crawlspace](/wiki/Crawlspace "Crawlspace"). Inside, she discovers Marie's charred corpse clutching a cellphone, which awakens. After pursuing and intercepting Beth, Marie weeps and murmurs, "Forgive me." Subsequently, Beth tells Jack that Marie might have brought her there to protect her.

At Laurel's [foster](/wiki/Foster_care "Foster care") home, Jack uncovers a [compact disc](/wiki/Compact_disc "Compact disc") from the [nanny cam](/wiki/Nanny_cam "Nanny cam") embedded in the eye of Laurel's teddy bear. The footage reveals Ellie incising Laurel with a knife in their bedroom. Marie entered shortly thereafter, discovered Ellie's abusiveness, and rushed Laurel to the hospital. Ellie, locked inside, began pressing wheezingly on the [inhaler](/wiki/Inhaler "Inhaler") but was overwhelmed and collapsed, facing the future curse's constituents ([millipedes](/wiki/Millipede "Millipede"), an [uncanny](/wiki/Uncanny "Uncanny") doll of a mother with baby in a [perambulator](/wiki/Baby_transport "Baby transport") (hallucinations), and the lullaby-esque music emanating from the teddy, audible during every victim's call), before dying from [asphyxia](/wiki/Asphyxia "Asphyxia") while dialing her mother's phone number. Laurel reveals that though Ellie injured her, she always provided candies. Realizing Ellie caused the curse, Jack drives to Beth's house, during which a colleague informs him of a new voicemail. After he enters, somebody knocks on the door. As he peers through the [peephole](/wiki/Peephole "Peephole"), a knife stabs through it, killing him. Ellie appears and attacks Beth, but Marie's spirit intervenes, binds Ellie in Jack's phone, and reconciles with Beth before evanescing. Jack's mouth spills a red candy and his cellphone auto-dials, revealing that the curse is still active.

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

*One Missed Call* was announced in 2005, before being officially [greenlit](/wiki/Greenlit "Greenlit") by [Warner Bros. Pictures](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Pictures "Warner Bros. Pictures") in early 2006, with Eric Valette signing as the film's [director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director").[[4]](#cite_note-UHM-4) The film began production in June[[4]](#cite_note-UHM-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) in [Atlanta, Georgia](/wiki/Atlanta,_Georgia "Atlanta, Georgia")[[6]](#cite_note-6) with [Edward Burns](/wiki/Edward_Burns "Edward Burns"), [Margaret Cho](/wiki/Margaret_Cho "Margaret Cho"), and [Shannyn Sossamon](/wiki/Shannyn_Sossamon "Shannyn Sossamon") signing on.[[7]](#cite_note-7) On August 3, [Ed Harris](/wiki/Ed_Harris "Ed Harris") and [Gabriel Byrne](/wiki/Gabriel_Byrne "Gabriel Byrne") both signed on to appear in the film but both withdrew due to unknown circumstances.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Sound designers used the voice of [Skid Row](/wiki/Skid_Row_(American_band) "Skid Row (American band)") front man [Sebastian Bach](/wiki/Sebastian_Bach "Sebastian Bach") in the hospital basement scene. The exact clip comes from Bach's scream at the beginning of "Midnight / Tornado", a song from the band's 1989 debut album, *[Skid Row](/wiki/Skid_Row_(Skid_Row_album) "Skid Row (Skid Row album)")*.

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Release")]

### Marketing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Marketing")]

In December 2007, the official website was launched[[9]](#cite_note-Official-9) as well as numerous websites running competitions to promote the film with the first prize being an [Apple](/wiki/Apple_Inc. "Apple Inc.") [iPhone](/wiki/IPhone "IPhone").[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Theatrical

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Theatrical")]

The film was intended for release on August 24, 2007, but was delayed until January 4, 2008.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) The song "[Life is Beautiful](/wiki/Life_Is_Beautiful_(Sixx:A.M._song) "Life Is Beautiful (Sixx:A.M. song)")" by [Sixx:A.M.](/wiki/Sixx:A.M. "Sixx:A.M.") was used in television advertisements for the film.

### Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Home media")]

The film was released on [DVD](/wiki/DVD "DVD"), [HD DVD](/wiki/HD_DVD "HD DVD"), and [Blu-ray](/wiki/Blu-ray "Blu-ray") on April 22, 2008.

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Reception")]

### Critical reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Critical reception")]

The film was not screened for critics.[[16]](#cite_note-16) On the [review aggregator](/wiki/Review_aggregator "Review aggregator") website [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes"), [0%](/wiki/List_of_films_with_a_0%25_rating_on_Rotten_Tomatoes "List of films with a 0% rating on Rotten Tomatoes") of 79 critics' reviews are positive, with an average rating of 2.6/10. The website's consensus reads: "One of the weakest entries in the [J-horror](/wiki/J-horror "J-horror") remake sweepstakes, *One Missed Call* is undone by bland performances and shopworn shocks". It was also awarded the Moldy Tomato Award for being the worst-reviewed film of 2008 and is rated the second worst film of the 2000s decade behind *[Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever](/wiki/Ballistic:_Ecks_vs._Sever "Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever")*.[[17]](#cite_note-17) [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), which uses a [weighted average](/wiki/Weighted_arithmetic_mean "Weighted arithmetic mean"), assigned the film a score of 24 out of 100, based on 14 critics, indicating "generally unfavorable" reviews.[[18]](#cite_note-18) Audiences surveyed by [CinemaScore](/wiki/CinemaScore "CinemaScore") gave the film a grade "D" on scale of A+ to F.

Despite being a remake of *Chakushin ari*, the film was strongly criticized for borrowing plot elements from and being similar to *[Scream](/wiki/Scream_(1996_film) "Scream (1996 film)")* (1996), *[Final Destination](/wiki/Final_Destination_(film) "Final Destination (film)")* (2000), *[The Ring](/wiki/The_Ring_(2002_film) "The Ring (2002 film)")* (2002), *[The Grudge](/wiki/The_Grudge "The Grudge")* (2004), *[Dark Water](/wiki/Dark_Water_(2005_film) "Dark Water (2005 film)")* (2005), and *[Pulse](/wiki/Pulse_(2006_film) "Pulse (2006 film)")* (2006).[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)

The film received two nominations — Burns for Choice Movie Actor: Horror/Thriller and Sossamon for Choice Movie Actress: Horror/Thriller — at the [2008 Teen Choice Awards](/wiki/2008_Teen_Choice_Awards "2008 Teen Choice Awards").[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Relationship with *One Missed Call* (2003 film)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=One_Missed_Call_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Relationship with One Missed Call (2003 film)")]

*One Missed Call* includes most of the scenes and characters that were in the [original 2003 film](/wiki/One_Missed_Call_(2003_film) "One Missed Call (2003 film)"). These include the [exorcism](/wiki/Exorcism "Exorcism") scene, where one of the characters is killed while filming the show *American Miracles*.[[9]](#cite_note-Official-9) The characters of Beth, Leann, Taylor, Jack, Brian, and Shelley are respectively based on the [original characters](/wiki/One_Missed_Call_(2003_film)#Cast "One Missed Call (2003 film)") Yumi, Yoko, Natsumi, Yamashita, Kenji, and Rina. Another reference is contained within the theatrical trailer; while Leann is walking down the street, a piece of the original ringtone from *One Missed Call* (2003) plays in the background until she falls from the overpass.[[9]](#cite_note-Official-9)
